ALL SPECIALTIES / PHARMACIST / WASHINGTON

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Washington Pharmacist License

Washington Pharmacist license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-02-25 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Pharmacist applications in Washington run through Washington Pharmacy Quality Assurance Commission. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official Transcripts - Sent directly from your educational institution.
  • Exam Score Reports - Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 4 to 6 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

  1. STEP 1

    Create a SAW Account

    Register for a SecureAccess Washington (SAW) account at secureaccess.wa.gov and add the 'DOH Health Professions' service to begin your application.

    Fee: $250 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Complete Online Application

    Log into your SAW account, access the DOH Health Professions service, and submit the pharmacist license application form, providing all required personal and professional details.

    Fee: $250 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Submit Supporting Documents

    Arrange for official transcripts from your ACPE-accredited pharmacy program to be sent directly to the Board. Upload or submit proof of 1,500 internship hours as required within the SAW portal or as directed by the Board.

    Fee: $250 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Pass Examinations

    Ensure your NAPLEX and MPJE scores are submitted directly from the National Association of Boards of Pharmacy (NABP) to the Washington State Board of Pharmacy.

    Fee: $250 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Pay Application Fees

    Remit all required application and examination fees as instructed through the SecureAccess Washington (SAW) portal to finalize your application submission.

    Fee: $250 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$250
Background check
$0 (included in application)
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$250

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
Washington Pharmacy Quality Assurance Commission
Phone
360-236-4700
Website
doh.wa.gov/licenses-permits-and-certificates/facilities-z/pharmacies-and-pharmaceutical-firms/commission-information
Application page
Open application

Renewal

Pharmacist renewal in Washington follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Annually on birthdate
Renewal fee
$140
Continuing education
30 hours every two years

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a Washington pharmacist license cost?

The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

Is the MPJE required in all states?

Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

Does Washington have reciprocity for pharmacists?

No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Washington. To work in Washington, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Washington?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

Can I start working in Washington while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$250

Processing time

4 to 6 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

NAPLEX and MPJE

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Annually on birthdate · $140

Licensing board

Washington Pharmacy Quality Assurance Commission
